what_it_does: Renders a single mobile-style workshop screen with a warm radial background, brand copy, a frosted status panel, map-like spots, metric chips, a glass menu label, and three footer icons generated at runtime.
observations: The page title is "Screen — the canvas (v1)".
It loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
The main surface is a div with classes "screen world".
The background is a radial gradient built from warmpole CSS variables.
Most elements are absolutely positioned inside the screen.
The visible copy frames the screen as "The Workshop" with a ledger and bench workflow.
The footer icon bar is populated by mapping plan, chat, and ledger through DNA.org.icon.
standouts: Warm full-screen canvas treatment using shared design tokens.
Layered glass/frost UI elements over a gradient field.
Runtime-generated icon row sourced from a DNA organism helper.
conventions: Uses design-token CSS variables for spacing, type, radius, and color.
Uses p-prefixed shared presentation classes such as p-frost, p-spot, p-glass, and p-hatch.
Keeps this file as a self-contained HTML mockup with inline, file-local CSS.
Uses inline style attributes for individual spot coordinates.
Uses a small inline script for runtime icon injection.
concerns: Layout is tightly coupled to fixed pixel positions and may be fragile outside the intended screen size.
The icon bar depends on a global DNA.org.icon object with no local fallback.
External font loading and root-relative asset paths are required for the intended rendering.
Inline spot coordinates make the spatial layer harder to reuse or inspect structurally.
notes: A compact workshop-themed screen mockup that treats a ledger interface as a layered visual canvas.
questions: What exact screen dimensions are assumed by the shared phone styles?
Are the p-spot markers meant to be interactive or only decorative?
What should happen if DNA.org.icon is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents a canvas-like mobile screen rather than a multi-page application or component library entry.
intention_signals: It seems intended to prototype a tactile workshop dashboard where operational status, spatial markers, and navigation sit on one atmospheric screen.
novelty: Combines workshop ledger copy, map-like spots, glass controls, and generated organism icons in a single static HTML screen.
contribution: Contributes a warm canvas screen pattern with layered status, marker, metric, menu, and icon-navigation elements that a fused system could retain as a visual substrate for workshop-ledger views.
summary_for_embedding: Single HTML mockup for a workshop ledger canvas screen with warm gradient background, frosted status card, spatial markers, metric chips, glass menu, and generated footer icons.
intention_for_embedding: The file was for exploring a mobile canvas treatment where workshop activity and ledger navigation are presented as layered visual elements.
